formula.description: Weighted formula for calculating color assignment confidence and tier placement

ai_confidence.description: AI model's confidence in color assignment based on frame analysis and metadata

authorship.description: Strength of authorial control over color (DP/director prestige and intentionality)
authorship.source: Cinematographer awards, director visual style reputation

cultural_recognition.description: Critical and institutional recognition of the film's visual achievement
cultural_recognition.source: Festival awards, critical canon membership, cinematography prizes

popularity.description: Public awareness and accessibility (tempered to avoid blockbuster bias)
popularity.source: TMDB popularity score, viewing figures, cultural penetration

notes: All component scores normalized to 0-100 before applying weights
Editorial boost can add up to +10 points (applied after formula)
Minimum score of 50 required for peripheral tier inclusion

canon.description: Essential masterworks that define the color's cinematic identity
canon.notes: Almost always requires editorial curation; formula alone rarely reaches 95+

core.description: Important works that strongly represent the color's character
core.notes: Mix of high-scoring algorithmic picks and editorial selections

strong.description: Solid examples with clear color identity and quality
strong.notes: Purely algorithmic ranking by score; no cap

peripheral.description: Valid but less distinctive examples; exploratory tier
peripheral.notes: Included for completeness; not featured prominently in UI

uncertain.description: Films below threshold; not displayed but retained for analysis
uncertain.notes: Used for debugging and future recalibration

canon.description: Manually curated rank order (1-30) based on cultural importance, influence, and exemplary status
canon.sorting: By tier_rank field (set in editorial override)
canon.notes: Each film assigned explicit rank; ties broken by year (older first)

core.description: Editorial tier_rank if present, else numeric score
sorting: If tier_rank exists: sort by tier_rank
If no tier_rank: sort by numeric_score descending
Ties broken by cultural_weight, then year (older first)
core.notes: Allows editorial intervention for key films while letting algorithm handle the rest

strong.sorting: By numeric_score descending, ties broken by cultural_weight, then year (older first)

peripheral.sorting: By numeric_score descending, ties broken by cultural_weight, then year (older first)

cultural_weight_signals.description: Factors that contribute to the cultural_recognition component (0-100 scale)

sight_and_sound_top_250.notes: Inclusion in Sight & Sound Greatest Films poll (any year)

tspdt_top_1000.notes: Inclusion in They Shoot Pictures Don't They top 1000

mubi_notebook_great_films.notes: Featured in MUBI Notebook's Great Films series

cannes_technical_prize.notes: Cannes Technical Grand Prize (often for cinematography)

arthouse_distribution.notes: Indicates curatorial validation and sustained cultural presence

criteria.notes: Bonus for non-English films to counteract Hollywood bias

editorial_boost.criteria: Manual boost applied by curator for films of exceptional visual significance not captured by metrics
editorial_boost.notes: Applied sparingly; requires written justification in editorial override file

authorship_signals.description: Factors that contribute to the authorship component (0-100 scale)

master_dp.description: DP with multiple Oscar nominations or iconic body of work

established_dp.description: DP with award nominations or sustained quality career

emerging_dp.description: DP with critical recognition but limited filmography

visual_auteur.description: Director known for strong visual authorship

visual_collaborator.description: Director with consistent DP partnerships and visual attention

production_design_award.description: Oscar/BAFTA production design win or nomination

popularity_normalization.description: How to convert raw popularity metrics to 0-100 score while avoiding blockbuster bias

tmdb_popularity.notes: Diminishing returns for extremely popular films

cap.notes: Prevents Marvel films from dominating purely on popularity

minimum_confidence.description: AI confidence must be ≥ 0.60 for any tier assignment
minimum_confidence.action_if_below: Flag for manual review; do not auto-assign

score_floor.description: Films scoring < 50 are excluded from public tiers
score_floor.action: Assign to 'uncertain' tier; retain in database for analysis

conditions: AI confidence < 0.70 and attempting canon tier
Score > 90 from unknown director/DP (potential data error)
Color shift detected in frame analysis (review primary color choice)
